                586.Origin_Intro_Laezel: You - Orpheus. Abomination. You have served your purpose, now - you will die.

                Metadata

                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: bbf335ae-0479-55a5-e4ee-ffbbdb3b57cdSpeaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h41303beage65bg49e6gac5dgacbb1b20bdc2Text UUID: 9cae6751-b76c-4bdc-970a-22f4e3dcc008Timeline Phase Index: 63Timeline Dialog Node UUID: bbf335ae-0479-55a5-e4ee-ffbbdb3b57cdTimeline Phase Duration: 22.4sTimeline Phase Shots: 9Timeline Location Changes: MouthOfPier - Orpheus Entrance 2 (1d7c98e4-47df-45f1-ac9f-614adbf02592) @ 542.11s; 5b90f002-a63b-4cbd-ad30-3f702e7a9416 (5b90f002-a63b-4cbd-ad30-3f702e7a9416) @ 544.29s; Landing - Laezel Confront Orpheus Walkin (1a4a2423-7eaa-42f8-a5c3-a5f4794513b6) @ 552.75sGroup ID: bbf335ae-0479-55a5-e4ee-ffbbdb3b57cdGroup Index: 0
                • +
                  475.Speaker 2: Do what you must. I cannot live in this skin any longer.note: Orpheus kneels nobly, offering himself for death.

                  Metadata

                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 972ffc2a-5751-aeee-5c00-b7e9f1c397d6Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h94372e06g6fc8g4c67g8436g7eb3e59d399eText UUID: 387a0fdb-26d2-46f0-a805-caad1f8e04efGroup ID: bbf335ae-0479-55a5-e4ee-ffbbdb3b57cdGroup Index: 1

                          570.ShadowheartIllusion: He sacrificed everything in order to save our lives, Lae'zel. That ought to count for something to you.END_RandomCompanion_PickedShadowheart

                          Flags and Tags

                          checktype Object | speaker 4 ShadowheartIllusion
                          • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        • +
                          503.Origin_Intro_Wyll: The Prince of the Comet has fallen. He sacrificed everything to stop the Grand Design. Does that mean nothing to you, Lae'zel?
                          END_RandomCompanion_PickedWyll

                          Flags and Tags

                          checktype Object | speaker 6 Origin_Intro_Wyll
                          • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                            151.Jump to Node 570

                            Metadata

                            Node Type: JumpUUID: 33fcf38c-2105-230d-ad4c-1294e445c16dJump Target: Node 570 (b58ff89c-f33b-ddbd-7349-c091f56dc4a7)Jump Target Point: 2
                        • +
                          242.Gale: He sacrificed his soul to save your people, Lae'zel. Surrendered his very being to prevent the Grand Design. I'd say it's a tad ungrateful to murder him for it.END_RandomCompanion_PickedGale

                          Flags and Tags

                          checktype Object | speaker 7 Gale
                          • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True
                          checktype Tag | speaker 7 Gale

                          Metadata

                        • +
                          408.Halsin: He paid the ultimate price, to save our lives. That should count for something, Lae'zel.END_RandomCompanion_PickedHalsin

                          Flags and Tags

                          checktype Object | speaker 9 Halsin
                          • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                          • See Node 151 Jump to Node 570
                        • +
                          244.Jaheira: He gave his life for ours, Lae'zel. You might give him the death he seeks without claiming it as some victory.END_RandomCompanion_PickedJaheira

                          Flags and Tags

                          checktype Object | speaker 10 Jaheira
                          • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True
                          checktype Tag | speaker 10 Jaheira

                          567.Speaker 11: He asks for death, Lae'zel. I think he has earned a noble and a quiet one, yes?END_RandomCompanion_PickedMinsc

                          654.Minthara: Do what you must, Lae'zel. But make it quick - we would have perished long ago without his protection.END_RandomCompanion_PickedMinthara

                            89.Player: He made the ultimate sacrifice to save our lives. Are you sure this is what you want, Lae'zel?

                            Metadata

                            Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 1f9f5789-4b1d-3d9d-967a-d045cb7f8987Speaker: 0 (Player)Text Handle: h5a798e01g68d9g4581gb34ag9b382ee70ea1Text UUID: 2bc97f97-0073-4112-aa4a-4c42ceb31d18Show Once: True
                            • +
                              262.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Save your sympathy. He is hshar'lak. Traitor, and ghaik.

                              Metadata

                              Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 5291deff-1c7a-7290-133c-68481db07218Speaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h78bfdf5fgdaf8g4954g94ffg11b00bf83bc9Text UUID: 3a13fefb-82a5-4657-a560-0a5a7b1a3fceTimeline Phase Index: 294Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 5291deff-1c7a-7290-133c-68481db07218Timeline Phase Duration: 6.35sTimeline Phase Shots: 2Timeline Location Changes: Landing - Laezel Confront Orpheus Walkin (1a4a2423-7eaa-42f8-a5c3-a5f4794513b6) @ 2823.21s
                              • +
                                182.Origin_Intro_Laezel: You made a promise to me when we freed him, and I intend to hold you to it, as I hold myself bound to my Queen. ORI_Laezel_State_VlaakithPathOrpheusAlly

                                Flags and Tags

                                checktype Global
                                • ORI_Laezel_State_VlaakithPathOrpheusAlly {0a57a2d4-f61d-84b2-547a-99afcdd8ba98} = True

                                • +

                                  • +

                                              625.Origin_Intro_Laezel: To slay a ghaik was my sworn duty. To kill Orpheus was my privilege.

                                                  129.Origin_Intro_Laezel: My people are leaving, and I must leave with them. I must call out to Tu'narath - my ascension's at hand.END_CombatOver_Event_NestedLaezelVlaakithAscension

                                                639.Speaker 2: You killed your ally to save a mind flayer? A poignant act, but a wasteful one. note: The player stands over Laezel's body.END_General_State_InclusionExcluded

                            675.Player: Prince Orpheus, why do you not resist? Githyanki

                            Flags and Tags

                            ruletagtype rule
                            • Githyanki {e49c027c-6ec6-4158-9afb-8b59236d10fd}

                            Text

                            Variant 1

                            combine=0
                            • Githyanki
                            Prince Orpheus, why do you not resist?
                            h88c03113g8805g405fg8d33g48a5b876f24b | 75904ba4-9f74-4244-bb9c-87390086d77f | stub

                            Variant 2

                            Orpheus, will you not fight back?

                  41.Speaker 2: Even when my time in the Prism stretched out like eternity - when escape seemed impossible - I never lost hope.

                  Metadata

                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 0c33e1fd-7e6e-1619-ebe5-8eb41c5b011eSpeaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h1fd17d3dg1712g4a5dg8ecbgd97b5cf4cbd2Text UUID: c19ea3c5-c69c-49a4-b9bf-74f6444b3aa6Timeline Phase Index: 199Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 0c33e1fd-7e6e-1619-ebe5-8eb41c5b011eTimeline Phase Duration: 9.43sTimeline Phase Shots: 2Timeline Location Changes: 3eda5c52-4beb-4a07-b0ff-2e98a1ce1ce5 (3eda5c52-4beb-4a07-b0ff-2e98a1ce1ce5) @ 1870.23s
                  • +
                    440.Speaker 2: I knew that my destiny was to liberate my people. To return to them triumphant.

                    Metadata

                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 8b96be57-2dd9-3799-9121-b3af86220a89Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h2c9e222fgddfcg4371ga957g3f5a5fc393ebText UUID: 5e56d3e7-33a6-463d-b736-2616b48b3bd4Timeline Phase Index: 289Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 8b96be57-2dd9-3799-9121-b3af86220a89Timeline Phase Duration: 35.18sTimeline Phase Shots: 9Timeline Location Changes: 1d219614-749f-4265-b938-2561bd3f6638 (1d219614-749f-4265-b938-2561bd3f6638) @ 2753.96s; 1d219614-749f-4265-b938-2561bd3f6638 (1d219614-749f-4265-b938-2561bd3f6638) @ 2757.35s; Landing - Orpheus Facing Player w Laezel - Turnabout (e1a872f4-e2cf-43e7-907b-986c9864d45b) @ 2759.95s; 442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29 (442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29) @ 2767s; 342b9433-5258-4414-8f7f-6cdc312a52e8 (342b9433-5258-4414-8f7f-6cdc312a52e8) @ 2777.4sGroup ID: 8b96be57-2dd9-3799-9121-b3af86220a89Group Index: 0
                    • +

                      Metadata

                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 4a41b488-7b1c-93c2-5a75-45587ef0b914Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h75cea55agcbabg44e6g8ee8g3b971b91b4fbText UUID: 34367366-2a6d-40fc-9aeb-3135a08aa55aGroup ID: 8b96be57-2dd9-3799-9121-b3af86220a89Group Index: 1
                      • +
                        525.Speaker 2: It seems I can only fulfil one part of my destiny - my people will be liberated, but I cannot return to them. Not like this.

                        Metadata

                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: a4c79a8d-2d90-fcc5-a08a-5ffdebc53043Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h6060538eg805fg4b90g8732g9f144c897a1aText UUID: cdf25151-c45d-4bbe-8377-fbfc829b0110Group ID: 8b96be57-2dd9-3799-9121-b3af86220a89Group Index: 2
                        • +
                          546.Speaker 2: You helped me destroy that abomination. Now help me destroy myself. You must kill me.note: Orpheus gets down on one knee and holds out his sword. The player takes it.

                          Metadata

                          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: ad6a648b-20bc-3c20-c24f-e3b86253d4a6Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h0ed7b1b6g624fg4f6dg83f9gc46c421bc4f0Text UUID: 6eb1745b-c98c-46b5-b46d-4098f1e5882aGroup ID: 8b96be57-2dd9-3799-9121-b3af86220a89Group Index: 3
                          • +

                              199.Speaker 2: Carry my hope, carry my burden. Call my dragons, Quulos and Quuthos, and ride to the Astral Sea. Destroy Vlaakith, release our people. Be our future and our legacy.

                                    27.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Vlaakith will fall, and we will plant a seed. From that seed, githyanki freedom will blossom.

                                  552.Origin_Intro_Laezel: You understand, then. We will never be free while our people are still bound by Vlaakith's chains.

                                  Metadata

                                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: ae344ed5-914b-bb51-b587-4585e839422dSpeaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: hcb856de0gca1fg4a74g814bg58c8f40c2b5fText UUID: 989a6f54-fb80-48e7-9e4c-29dbf5414b16Timeline Phase Index: 168Timeline Dialog Node UUID: ae344ed5-914b-bb51-b587-4585e839422dTimeline Phase Duration: 16.86sTimeline Phase Shots: 2Timeline Location Changes: d628500f-99a1-4f0d-8590-41935e216086 (d628500f-99a1-4f0d-8590-41935e216086) @ 1535.71sGroup ID: ae344ed5-914b-bb51-b587-4585e839422dGroup Index: 0
                                  • +
                                    117.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Vlaakith will fall, and we will plant a seed. From that seed, githyanki freedom will blossom.

                                    Metadata

                                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 267f6cf3-ebea-78cb-c30a-d316321f372aSpeaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h1190da48g4f50g4aa3g8dedg0ecd289f2801Text UUID: 9191fb73-2a3d-4081-9a39-4024c6651a9aGroup ID: ae344ed5-914b-bb51-b587-4585e839422dGroup Index: 1
                                    • +

                                • +
                                  106.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Duty... All my life, I've travelled in its slipstream, not once questioning its path. In its service, I came here. And now, in its service, I leave. ORI_State_PartneredWithLaezel

                                  Flags and Tags

                                  checktype Object | speaker 0 Player

                                  • +
                                    691.Origin_Intro_Laezel: I will carry your hope, Prince Orpheus, and I will carry your burden. But to that burden I must add my own - the loss of those I leave behind.

                                  115.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Mother Gith battled for liberty - but it was only here that I learned what it meant to be adored, to be free.END_GameFinale_State_LaezelInFaerun

                                  • +
                                    394.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Yes, I will stay.

                                    Metadata

                                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 7ec03f3e-792c-1d33-929a-cbafb9847fb7Speaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h96f0ce4dgc275g4d87gaf59ge6ac74bcb2f1Text UUID: 9c864b28-7629-4a1f-b744-de50a786e8f7Group ID: 25c4adc0-ec5c-f9fa-fc91-9132075cac1bGroup Index: 1
                                    • +
                                      572.Speaker 2: Then my destiny is undone. Until someone can take up my cause, the githyanki will remain chained.

                                      Metadata

                                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: b5f6e580-8a82-5e7c-d202-3346ab5cc91fSpeaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: hbf6574ddgdfe8g4889gadc0g2cfa8583e393Text UUID: 42cf9187-243e-4aa4-9aec-2c19ca9f252eTimeline Phase Index: 78Timeline Dialog Node UUID: b5f6e580-8a82-5e7c-d202-3346ab5cc91fTimeline Phase Duration: 20.59sTimeline Phase Shots: 5Timeline Location Changes: 442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29 (442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29) @ 687.67sGroup ID: b5f6e580-8a82-5e7c-d202-3346ab5cc91fGroup Index: 0
                                      • +
                                        785.Speaker 2: I found freedom, and then lost myself to this horror for nothing. All that remains is to die.

                                        Metadata

                                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: fe73767a-358a-aa43-6d84-6b8ab29d1f55Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: ha7413f36g73d9g45fag8e2agff3baa4e7f03Text UUID: 6f38448a-eac7-4d2f-9d82-02c45a1a00f0Group ID: b5f6e580-8a82-5e7c-d202-3346ab5cc91fGroup Index: 1
                                        • •

                                    287.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Mother Gith battled for liberty - but it was only here, with you, that I learned what it meant to be free.END_GameFinale_State_LaezelInFaerun

                                    Flags and Tags

                                    settype Global
                                    • END_GameFinale_State_LaezelInFaerun {3cb6879c-f4e7-bb31-584a-63bea28ecb75} = True

                                    Metadata

                                    • +
                                      566.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Yes - I will stay. My destiny is for neither Vlaakith nor Orpheus to decree. It is mine.

                                      Metadata

                                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: b35d0239-c30c-1284-2342-375f4c499b1aSpeaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: hb78229d5gbca4g4ecfg93a4gacc3031deaf0Text UUID: 234458fd-f3c8-4084-b85e-9dc7535d7691Group ID: 5a8492b7-dfea-c8ae-f221-e6bfeb1ca4c3Group Index: 1
                                      • See Node 572 (Speaker 2) Then my destiny is undone. Until someone can take up my cause, the githyanki will remain chained.
                                • +

                                  Metadata

                                  • +
                                    336.Origin_Intro_Laezel: I have already chosen a way - the way of Orpheus. Vlaakith will fall, and I will plant a seed. From that seed, githyanki freedom will blossom.END_GameFinale_State_LaezelOrpheusPath

                                    Flags and Tags

                                    settype Global
                                    • END_GameFinale_State_LaezelOrpheusPath {a74531fa-52d8-13e8-562f-d3a528e73f82} = True

                                    • +
                                      660.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Your will is strong, ra'stil. Your name will be etched in our slates. You will be called Mla'ghir - liberator.

                                      Metadata

                                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: d4a3b1ae-01c7-e8ae-16b9-7c6be2118dfdSpeaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h284f3b34g4eacg4ef8g81d0ga7ae06336446Text UUID: fafc4654-cc2f-43ff-90f1-da74eb57af0bGroup ID: 6daf78bf-77a0-ca8f-4539-60c5e221f7abGroup Index: 1

                                  339.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Duty... All my life, I've travelled in its slipstream, not once questioning its path. In its service, I came here. And now, in its service, I leave. END_GameFinale_State_LaezelOrpheusPath

                                  Flags and Tags

                                  settype Global
                                  • END_GameFinale_State_LaezelOrpheusPath {a74531fa-52d8-13e8-562f-d3a528e73f82} = True

                          • +
                            157.Speaker 2: Do it quickly. I cannot live in this skin a moment longer.note: Orpheus kneels before the player as a mind flayer.

                                107.Narrator: *You promised your Queen you would do this.*!ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPathORI_Laezel_State_AcceptedVlaakithsFinalMissionAvatarLae'zel

                                • +
                                  688.Narrator: *To slay a ghaik was your sworn duty, to execute Orpheus will be an honour indeed. You will surely ascend, and ascend in glory.*

                                  Metadata

                                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: dc587fba-663a-7e2d-9985-88180c4e9361Speaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: h999d1999ga59fg49b3g9658g2ca5edf2cbdfText UUID: 623a7d93-6b1a-482a-a653-787d13f00c23Timeline Phase Index: 236Timeline Dialog Node UUID: dc587fba-663a-7e2d-9985-88180c4e9361Timeline Phase Duration: 20.05sTimeline Phase Shots: 4Timeline Location Changes: 442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29 (442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29) @ 2206.08sGroup ID: dc587fba-663a-7e2d-9985-88180c4e9361Group Index: 0
                                  • +
                                    338.Narrator: *But the rebel prince is the only reason you are still alive. It was his power that protected you from the Netherbrain. Does he deserve to die?*

                                    Metadata

                                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 6dfa4860-95ae-9ac1-58ba-e82d689520b1Speaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: he6dfc95dgdaf2g4047g9477g5872b3ab117eText UUID: b4da846f-f96e-4a4b-a148-1e7621b35091Group ID: dc587fba-663a-7e2d-9985-88180c4e9361Group Index: 1

                                    • +
                                      790.Player: Get up, Orpheus. For what you've done, you deserve to live.

                                      Metadata

                                      Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: fee6c592-5167-b47b-be99-d000dc11c5feSpeaker: 0 (Player)Text Handle: h7dfbdc90geb0dg455agbd92g25b4036f0989Text UUID: 83852818-aede-44a9-8163-77c9acf5c98aShow Once: True
                                      • +
                                        622.Speaker 2: I will not be ghaik! I did what I did to save my people.

                                        Metadata

                                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: c870f6ab-3026-0818-f2aa-99596c844152Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: hb489b02dgc223g4f01g8bd1g90b3e1efdd16Text UUID: 005b39f7-218d-4c32-9c91-bb9932d47b59Timeline Phase Index: 125Timeline Dialog Node UUID: c870f6ab-3026-0818-f2aa-99596c844152Timeline Phase Duration: 7.65sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Location Changes: 442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29 (442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29) @ 1160.19s
                                        • +
                                          696.Speaker 2: The revolution against Vlaakith will come. Perhaps some day you might see truth, Lae'zel, and join it.!ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPathAvatarLae'zel

                                          • +
                                            359.Speaker 2: I trust that our people will have their freedom, but now I must have mine. Release my soul to the Astral Seas, while I still have one to call my own.

                                            Metadata

                                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 759c1fd8-462b-5db4-4e2d-979eab777685Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: hc3e598fbgbfaag490dg8ce3gb2d581d4b393Text UUID: 2d3b0456-e064-4020-a4d6-ddd0cba38c07Group ID: df92603e-85c2-5de2-447a-237e35c15885Group Index: 1
                                            • +

                                              206.Player: Say what you will - I have no intention of killing you.

                                              Metadata

                                              Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 4238ec48-685e-76d6-d8a7-8ac6d537cf12Speaker: 0 (Player)Text Handle: h3c18c04ag0274g4ffdg95a6g70dbe5b7d9c8Text UUID: c6a61c7c-d55d-4cde-8e63-ad6b0d214bb8Show Once: True
                                              • +
                                                444.Speaker 2: Then I will do once more what must be done.

                                                Metadata

                                                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 8ca0ab39-645e-e329-d1c9-c5cc0fd1494aSpeaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h0978e41bgeba9g4fa6gbc9dgfb4b5a1f1c93Text UUID: 59fae380-6397-48fa-9353-15c3ce591a1dTimeline Phase Index: 264Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 8ca0ab39-645e-e329-d1c9-c5cc0fd1494aTimeline Phase Duration: 13.97sTimeline Phase Shots: 4Timeline Location Changes: 442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29 (442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29) @ 2491.24sGroup ID: 8ca0ab39-645e-e329-d1c9-c5cc0fd1494aGroup Index: 0
                                                • +
                                                  358.Speaker 2: It was an honour to fight beside you to end the Grand Design.

                                                  Metadata

                                                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 7574f9c3-4efe-0b15-baed-855532e706d8Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h81821f87g9d9ag4c56ga75dga2dc42bf1376Text UUID: c836b240-5545-4a4c-91ed-abeb8b8cb88eGroup ID: 8ca0ab39-645e-e329-d1c9-c5cc0fd1494aGroup Index: 1
                                                  • +
                                                    152.Speaker 2: Gith'ka tavkim krash'ht.note: Orpheus kills himself plunging his sword into his stomach, and speaks his last words from a kneeling position before collapsingEND_General_State_OrpheusDefeated

                                                            • +
                                                              423.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Zhak vo'n'fynh duj - source of my joy. Without you, I wouldn't have known warmth - only ice and fire.ORI_Laezel_State_CompletedRomanceORI_State_PartneredWithLaezel

                                                              Flags and Tags

                                                              checktype Global

                                                                203.Origin_Intro_Laezel: I can never forget you. Your name will be etched in our slates. You will be called Mla'ghir - liberator.

                                                                Metadata

                                                                • +

                                                          454.ShadowheartIllusion: He was as noble in spirit as he was in bloodline. We're in his debt.END_RandomCompanion_PickedShadowheart

                                                        • +
                                                          541.Astarion: Well, that was a kindness. Can you imagine living like that? Urgh.ORI_Astarion_State_BecameVampireLordEND_RandomCompanion_PickedAstarion

                                                          Flags and Tags

                                                        • +
                                                          145.Astarion: He really did give everything for his people - and for us. I hope he finally knows peace.!ORI_Astarion_State_BecameVampireLordEND_RandomCompanion_PickedAstarion

                                                          Flags and Tags

                                                        • +
                                                          665.Origin_Intro_Wyll: A brave and noble prince. May the stories of his sacrifice travel far and wide.END_RandomCompanion_PickedWyll

                                                          Flags and Tags

                                                        • +
                                                          346.Gale: A kinder fate than to live as one's sworn enemy, though no less a pity for it. At least in legend, the Prince of the Comet will live forever. END_RandomCompanion_PickedGale

                                                          Flags and Tags

                                                          checktype Object | speaker 7 Gale
                                                          • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                                                          Metadata

                                                          • See Node 761 (No Speaker) (no line text)
                                                        • +
                                                          451.Halsin: He did not hesitate. A true sacrifice, and a noble spirit. May his spirit find its way home.END_RandomCompanion_PickedHalsin

                                                          Flags and Tags

                                                          checktype Object | speaker 9 Halsin
                                                          • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                                                          Metadata

                                                          • See Node 761 (No Speaker) (no line text)
                                                        • +
                                                          256.Jaheira: Imprisoned most of his life, and he freely gave what little remained to save his people. Rare, that a hero actually lives up to their prophecy.END_RandomCompanion_PickedJaheira

                                                          Flags and Tags

                                                          checktype Object | speaker 10 Jaheira
                                                          • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                                                          • See Node 761 (No Speaker) (no line text)
                                                        • +
                                                          249.Speaker 11: The princeling deserved a better end than this. We shall remember him, Boo.END_RandomCompanion_PickedMinsc

                                                          Flags and Tags

                                                          checktype Object | speaker 11 Speaker 11
                                                          • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                                                          • See Node 761 (No Speaker) (no line text)
                                                        • +
                                                          393.Minthara: We would not have survived any of this without him. May he find peace.END_RandomCompanion_PickedMinthara

                                                          Flags and Tags

                                                          checktype Object | speaker 12 Minthara
                                                          • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                                                        • +
                                                          703.Origin_Intro_Laezel: As noble in death as he was in life. His sacrifice will not be forgotten. END_RandomCompanion_PickedLae'zel

                                                          Flags and Tags

                                        • +
                                          607.Speaker 2: The rest is up to them. Someone else must rise within the ranks to lead the revolution against Vlaakith.

                                          Metadata

                                          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: c0ea49c7-4d51-f02d-53e1-90042bb86853Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h26c9447eg2ce1g4438g89b0g5ceb7f6cd670Text UUID: ed9342e2-2fbd-4805-bae3-ca2d2be5e6cfTimeline Phase Index: 124Timeline Dialog Node UUID: c0ea49c7-4d51-f02d-53e1-90042bb86853Timeline Phase Duration: 19.8sTimeline Phase Shots: 3Timeline Location Changes: 442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29 (442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29) @ 1148.87sGroup ID: c0ea49c7-4d51-f02d-53e1-90042bb86853Group Index: 0
                                          • +
                                            726.Speaker 2: Give me my freedom from this form, release my soul to the Astral Seas while I still have one to call my own.

                                            Metadata

                                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: ebc4ccde-fbbd-a8dc-e802-9f1c94ef32aeSpeaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h4f10516bgeea5g4b8eg9109gcb3720a14aa0Text UUID: 25e685d0-6567-406f-9a78-a224ff0c1dcaGroup ID: c0ea49c7-4d51-f02d-53e1-90042bb86853Group Index: 1
                                            • See Node 206 (Player) Say what you will - I have no intention of killing you.
                                            • +

                                                  533.Speaker 2: You mean well, but you do not understand.

                                                  Metadata

                  412.Origin_Intro_Karlach: It's all right. I knew what I was doing.

                  Metadata

                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 8338ed61-2694-db01-d402-ca34b77db703Speaker: 8 (Origin_Intro_Karlach)Text Handle: h757380b4gf8d1g400agbacfge5246f690cb0Text UUID: 2f5e1853-b967-4d5a-b16e-6c019ed2b3bcTimeline Phase Index: 45Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 8338ed61-2694-db01-d402-ca34b77db703Timeline Phase Duration: 3.23sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Unmapped Attributes: stub
                  • +
                    664.Speaker 2: I will ensure that all githyanki know your name - and your sacrifice. What you have done today will start a fire that rages across the Astral Plane.

                    Metadata

                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: d634fa52-e8ea-280e-4b15-87450dcdc40cSpeaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h24445dcbgf865g4ba4gab0cgca14b9934132Text UUID: ab8c4897-527c-41e6-a5c1-1e0fb4e35c6cTimeline Phase Index: 72Timeline Dialog Node UUID: d634fa52-e8ea-280e-4b15-87450dcdc40cTimeline Phase Duration: 29.21sTimeline Phase Shots: 8Timeline Location Changes: bbc7f1a9-54b8-470d-a2c0-59bae5b0a1d3 (bbc7f1a9-54b8-470d-a2c0-59bae5b0a1d3) @ 632.26s; ec816043-c4ea-4727-b48b-ae08038fa2b4 (ec816043-c4ea-4727-b48b-ae08038fa2b4) @ 643.31s; 1d219614-749f-4265-b938-2561bd3f6638 (1d219614-749f-4265-b938-2561bd3f6638) @ 647.88s; Landing - Orpheus Facing Player w Mindflayer Karlach (1cf9db49-560a-466b-aae3-7032b759e272) @ 651.27s; Landing - Player w Mindflayer Karlach QHOLD (aea71c69-9a69-4ce1-bb3a-816eb77b4875) @ 660.89sGroup ID: d634fa52-e8ea-280e-4b15-87450dcdc40cGroup Index: 0
                    • +
                      450.Speaker 2: With their lost prince returned, my people will burn away Vlaakith's corruption. And it is all thanks to you - the illithid who defied a Netherbrain.

                      Metadata

                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 8d75459d-3cf8-f087-9ebf-1227aa743fcaSpeaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h5767a83dgd69dg4488gb125g3826eb192726Text UUID: 35838c92-677e-4940-8e1a-42bd2d29a751Group ID: d634fa52-e8ea-280e-4b15-87450dcdc40cGroup Index: 1
                      • +
                        626.Player: You did good, Karlach.

                        Metadata

                        Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: caa43afe-04dc-6127-5907-e70700a9068eSpeaker: 0 (Player)Text Handle: h5c55f365gbd6cg4290gb3eag1f75defbe89dText UUID: 51fe2c14-7f99-4606-9c80-964db99fe9d9Show Once: True
                        • +
                          786.Origin_Intro_Karlach: I did, didn't I?

                          Metadata

                          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: feb389a3-482f-8721-5ec3-cd62cbd67bbfSpeaker: 8 (Origin_Intro_Karlach)Text Handle: hd40d568fg21fbg4e78ga794g76373c1a3e33Text UUID: ec6463de-ae2c-4b3d-805c-bb44a472cf17Timeline Phase Index: 99Timeline Dialog Node UUID: feb389a3-482f-8721-5ec3-cd62cbd67bbfTimeline Phase Duration: 2.26sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Location Changes: Landing - Player w Mindflayer Karlach (5528e48e-29e6-4a02-9ad9-8950f9ae3768) @ 908.19s
                          • +
                            373.Origin_Intro_Karlach: I saved the fucking world. And I think I might live long enough to tell the tale, too.

                            Metadata

                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 78e99074-3a67-8e76-ec39-94e51e39e53cSpeaker: 8 (Origin_Intro_Karlach)Text Handle: h100411b0g8d27g44b1g9953g0c38ac58dc55Text UUID: fe46f4ad-389b-487c-892e-806a90f716d0Timeline Phase Index: 254Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 78e99074-3a67-8e76-ec39-94e51e39e53cTimeline Phase Duration: 6.93sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                            • +
                              495.Speaker 2: I thank you, my liberator, my saviour.

                              Metadata

                              Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 9d7a7802-1b17-d210-d1b1-b8f255b82856Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: h50cef2bcg8b7bg41ceg943ag6c07d8445a6aText UUID: a483791f-044d-4fcc-89c3-115ab886a054Timeline Phase Index: 16Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 9d7a7802-1b17-d210-d1b1-b8f255b82856Timeline Phase Duration: 4.67sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Location Changes: Landing - Orpheus Facing Player w Mindflayer Karlach (1cf9db49-560a-466b-aae3-7032b759e272) @ 131.16s
                              • +
                                539.Narrator: *He may have no reason to thank you, yet. You promised your Queen that you would end him. Now that the Netherbrain is defeated, the moment is yours.*!ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPathORI_Laezel_State_AcceptedVlaakithsFinalMissionAvatarLae'zel

                                Flags and Tags

                                • +
                                  616.Narrator: *Kill Orpheus, and you will ascend. Let him live, and you will never know Vlaakith's honour. You will be hshar'lak - traitor to your people, traitor to your Queen.*

                                  Metadata

                                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: c4653327-531f-27b0-0a3a-cddc829a27b8Speaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: h71f18c0bg234eg4b07ga7e5g4ba1ab7ac56fText UUID: 38eb65d3-668f-42a2-a0d1-0a95650628d8Timeline Phase Index: 120Timeline Dialog Node UUID: c4653327-531f-27b0-0a3a-cddc829a27b8Timeline Phase Duration: 12.82sTimeline Phase Shots: 3

                                          • +
                                            708.Gale: The depth of your commitment to your cause truly knows no bounds. For him to have survived a Netherbrain only to be ended by your hand... well, it is an end, I suppose. END_RandomCompanion_PickedGale

                                            Flags and Tags

                                            checktype Object | speaker 7 Gale

                                          • +
                                            740.Halsin: I cannot say I understand the reason for his death, but nature will reclaim him all the same. END_RandomCompanion_PickedHalsin

                                            Flags and Tags

                                          • +
                                            102.Jaheira: A decisive end to the one whose powers protected you. I hope whatever you gain from his sacrifice is worth it. END_RandomCompanion_PickedJaheira

                                            Flags and Tags

                                          • +
                                            85.Speaker 11: Boo is conflicted. He liked Orpheus, but he likes you more, and killing him seems to have made you happy. END_RandomCompanion_PickedMinsc

                              • +
                                457.Narrator: *He may have no reason to thank you, yet. Now that the Netherbrain is defeated, the moment is yours.*!ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPathAvatarLae'zel

                                Flags and Tags

                                checktype Global
                                • ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPath {97244ccc-a6b5-403e-9cfe-a3502eb4af56} = False
                                checktype Tag | speaker 0 Player
                                • Avatar {306b9b05-1057-4770-aa17-01af21acd650} = True
                                • Lae'zel {b5682d1d-c395-489c-9675-1f9b0c328ea5} = True

                                Metadata

                                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 8f7a8044-3dbc-d38f-63e6-9260b0baaffcSpeaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: hdeb98946g9b64g444cg849cg4e5efa658508Text UUID: ff12ece0-75fb-4abd-90cb-534c09e778d9Timeline Phase Index: 67Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 8f7a8044-3dbc-d38f-63e6-9260b0baaffcTimeline Phase Duration: 7.23sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                                • See Node 616 (Narrator) *Kill Orpheus, and you will ascend. Let him live, and you will never know Vlaakith's honour. You will be hshar'lak - traitor to your people, traitor to your Queen.*
                              • +
                                505.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Save your thanks, traitor. They are hardly worth the breath you waste to speak them. !ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPath!AvatarLae'zel

                                • +
                                  631.Speaker 2: You are blinded by your loyalty, child. Still, I cannot blame you. A false queen's lies may yet ring true to honourable ears.

                          • +
                            247.Origin_Intro_Karlach: Don't worry about me. I'm... happy.

                            Metadata

                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 4f87092c-3a69-917d-18ee-538aa4796d54Speaker: 8 (Origin_Intro_Karlach)Text Handle: hd0033989gd948g4aa2g8873g18e0cd636556Text UUID: 1d07b352-281d-45d2-9427-4f53472f2944Timeline Phase Index: 305Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 4f87092c-3a69-917d-18ee-538aa4796d54Timeline Phase Duration: 5.3sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                            • See Node 373 (Origin_Intro_Karlach) I saved the fucking world. And I think I might live long enough to tell the tale, too.
                      • +

                      624.Speaker 2: I will ensure that all githyanki know your name - and your sacrifice. What you have done today will start a fire that rages across the Astral Plane.

                      Metadata

                      • +
                        604.Narrator: *Can your people really celebrate you in such a form as this? Your sworn duty was to slay a ghaik, not become one.*!ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPathAvatarLae'zel

                        749.Origin_Intro_Laezel: The fire will rage, but you will not be there to see it. The Astral Seas have no place for you, hshar'lak.!ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPath!AvatarLae'zel

                                          135.Speaker 2: Even as she would have struck me down, I cannot celebrate this child's death. Her devotion to her queen was without fault. It was Vlaakith who failed her. END_General_State_InclusionExcluded

                                          Flags and Tags

                                          settype Object | speaker 3 Origin_Intro_Laezel

                                                    • +
                                                      360.Speaker 2: I shall not speak of Lae'zel's final act. Her story will be written as it was before - a warrior, who ended the Grand Design. END_CombatOver_State_PlayerKilledLaezel

                                                              647.Narrator: *The hope in his voice is sincere, fervent even. But in spite of your desire to believe him, you know the truth. You are ghaik. You will not be welcome in the Astral Sea, and nor will he.*Full-Illithid

                                                              Flags and Tags

                                                                  323.Speaker 2: I will not say I'm pleased with your choice - but I will wish you many fortunes. Whatever may come, you are still T'lak'ma Ghir - Sister in Freedom.Full-Illithid

                                                                  Flags and Tags

                                                                  Variant 2

                                                                  I will not say I'm pleased with your choice - but I will wish you many fortunes. Whatever may come, you are still T'lak'ma Ghir - Sister in Freedom.
                                                                  ha8274ad0gb20eg4542g9933g4ab6fc21c9c3 | 20dc8bf1-dda2-4377-a7ae-bafaa5336cc1 | stub

                                                                                        51.Player: You are a mind flayer now. You have never been so wise, nor strong, nor cool. Embrace it.

                                                                                        Metadata

                                                                                        Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 105a9452-ad94-ad16-66d7-ee4c3c049479Speaker: 0 (Player)Text Handle: h55c66bd3g157eg4811g9dc0gf49a56cfaa37Text UUID: b515aec2-7a24-4ea6-8da5-c354fe62271dShow Once: True
                                                                                        • +

                                                                                      • +
                                                                                        313.Player: You will survive, but you will be ready to take your life if you feel the slightest loss of control.

                                                                                        Metadata

                                                                                        Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 653ede3d-7c6f-0f92-68c0-410bfe547fadSpeaker: 0 (Player)Text Handle: hf3d6b8ceg0f7ag4d15g8286g0f110667d5a8Text UUID: 2d00d1a6-3442-4af3-a8d7-37b37377ba9dShow Once: True
                                                                                        • +
                                                                                          9.Narrator: *You have thwarted the illithid Grand Design. You have honoured your people, you have secured their future. Your duty is fulfilled.*

                                                                                          Metadata

                                                                                          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 0236bb54-c31d-d9d2-8a61-255744144e97Speaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: ha5ce97a3gcb90g439fga751g23230c146412Text UUID: 3e0448d0-eb5f-42c3-9f7d-22f7db2d24e7Timeline Phase Index: 209Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 0236bb54-c31d-d9d2-8a61-255744144e97Timeline Phase Duration: 9.84sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                                                                                          • +
                                                                                            498.Narrator: *Your fate will be what you make of it. Now, you are your own master.*

                                                                                            Metadata

                                                              • +
                                                                582.Origin_Intro_Laezel: I say farewell to a lover. And I say farewell to you, a staunch ally and... friend.

                                                                Metadata

                                                                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: b9f831ad-97c2-5867-1bb9-f4294aac4833Speaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: hadcac5e6g971dg4a17g87adgef894265c9e6Text UUID: f589e832-3be4-4058-b73a-bada8ccb2b7eGroup ID: f1c70730-5b07-2921-0ca5-ca6ef84dec91Group Index: 1
                                                                • See Node 214 (Origin_Intro_Laezel) I can never forget you. Your name will be etched in our slates. You will be called Mla'ghir - liberator.
                                                            • +
                                                              669.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Chk - then stay, turn your back on your people. But you will never truly be free while our people are still bound by Vlaakith's chains.Githyanki

                                                              • +
                                                                587.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Mother Gith battled for liberty - but it was only here, with you, that I learned what it meant to be free.END_GameFinale_State_LaezelInFaerun

                                                                Flags and Tags

                                                                settype Global
                                                                • END_GameFinale_State_LaezelInFaerun {3cb6879c-f4e7-bb31-584a-63bea28ecb75} = True

                                                                Metadata

                                                                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: bc0a2271-fff0-116a-5a2b-574f4f30e437Speaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: he83fc90agcc0dg4114ga08bgf8904ced10d4Text UUID: d29a31c3-939d-44e5-b430-7071ae8c1756Timeline Phase Index: 131Timeline Dialog Node UUID: bc0a2271-fff0-116a-5a2b-574f4f30e437Timeline Phase Duration: 8.66sTimeline Phase Shots: 1Timeline Location Changes: d628500f-99a1-4f0d-8590-41935e216086 (d628500f-99a1-4f0d-8590-41935e216086) @ 1211.9s
                                                                • +
                                                                  627.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Orpheus - I'll remain here. My destiny is for neither you nor Vlaakith to set. It is mine alone.

                                                                  Metadata

                                                                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: caef1359-d68b-16ae-cd34-04bcad5a01beSpeaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h4f157091ga604g48f6g954cgb7676b8f7fb2Text UUID: 5401c922-2201-4aff-b0c2-c31d0c9eab41Timeline Phase Index: 10Timeline Dialog Node UUID: caef1359-d68b-16ae-cd34-04bcad5a01beTimeline Phase Duration: 21.2sTimeline Phase Shots: 3Timeline Location Changes: 442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29 (442ed7d3-6c01-4ffb-9f53-bdfff50a5a29) @ 64.39sGroup ID: caef1359-d68b-16ae-cd34-04bcad5a01beGroup Index: 0
                                                                  • +
                                                                    510.Speaker 2: I will not say I'm pleased with your choice - but I will wish you good fortunes. Whatever may come, you are still T'lak'ma Ghir - Sister in Freedom.

                                                                    Metadata

                                                                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: a12a926a-531f-1cb3-fbcc-12728af301a3Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: hb472ff9bgadcag4503g8c1cg5e698d029fdcText UUID: 2dbeacdd-f696-4f2f-8f86-19a61e290af4Group ID: caef1359-d68b-16ae-cd34-04bcad5a01beGroup Index: 1
                                                                    • +
                                                                      52.Speaker 2: Quulos!note: |A dragon lands. Orpheus mounts it.|

                                                                      Metadata

                                                              • +
                                                                492.Origin_Intro_Laezel: I have already chosen a way - the way of Orpheus. Vlaakith will fall, and we will plant a seed. From that seed, githyanki freedom will blossom.

                                                                Metadata

                                                                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 9c1608f4-7709-2ba6-4053-e2a3227def9fSpeaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: h9590a1c3ga6deg43f6g95dbgd625dc660a94Text UUID: 92f39798-42f1-42d3-8ac6-6d7869251c79Timeline Phase Index: 28Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 9c1608f4-7709-2ba6-4053-e2a3227def9fTimeline Phase Duration: 21.8sTimeline Phase Shots: 2Timeline Location Changes: d628500f-99a1-4f0d-8590-41935e216086 (d628500f-99a1-4f0d-8590-41935e216086) @ 242.88sGroup ID: 9c1608f4-7709-2ba6-4053-e2a3227def9fGroup Index: 0Addressed Speaker: 0
                                                                • +
                                                                  540.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Your will is strong, ra'stil. Your name will be etched in our slates. You will be called Mla'ghir - liberator.

                                                                  Metadata

                                                                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: ab54c137-5d4d-7daf-7480-57fa1c0e6a8cSpeaker: 3 (Origin_Intro_Laezel)Text Handle: haba02926g480cg44efga22eg377891d72127Text UUID: 7eb55a21-3fec-491a-9c4a-57ab0c5f81a3Group ID: 9c1608f4-7709-2ba6-4053-e2a3227def9fGroup Index: 1Addressed Speaker: 0

                                                            • +
                                                              628.Origin_Intro_Laezel: It... it's not meant to be. You are ghaik. As noble as you are, this is a threshold I cannot cross.

                                                              219.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Yes. Yes. Mother Gith battled for liberty - but it was only here that I learned what it meant to be free.

                                                            • +
                                                              757.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Then I must go with the Prince. I will never be free while my people are still bound by Vlaakith's chains.END_GameFinale_State_LaezelOrpheusPath

                        32.Speaker 2: With their lost prince returned, my people will burn away Vlaakith's corruption. And it is all thanks to you - the illithid who defied a Netherbrain.

                        Metadata

                              144.Speaker 2: They have endured an age of darkness and tyranny, though most did not know it. When my war against Vlaakith is done, everything will change.

                              Metadata

                              Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 310008a5-0fea-de85-56c7-971cdd9b73a8Speaker: 2 (Speaker 2)Text Handle: ha9392befg41c4g4d24gbd83g71daf643445fText UUID: dd0df8a9-cfae-4eef-b075-fd4ef9f53fa7Timeline Phase Index: 190Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 310008a5-0fea-de85-56c7-971cdd9b73a8Timeline Phase Duration: 11.42sTimeline Phase Shots: 2Timeline Location Changes: 342b9433-5258-4414-8f7f-6cdc312a52e8 (342b9433-5258-4414-8f7f-6cdc312a52e8) @ 1794.52s

                          • +
                            130.Player: To be honest, I wish I'd made you do it.

                            Metadata

                            Node Type: TagQuestionUUID: 2a5b351e-5c23-be31-367f-be06ba168658Speaker: 0 (Player)Text Handle: hd8a056f8gef86g4e99ga7f4g5e68a80f6db2Text UUID: af16da53-a0fe-46d7-a1e6-76eaaaf3003fShow Once: True
                            • +
                              407.Speaker 2: It is understandable that you regret this... horror you have endured. But you made the right choice - my people need their prince.

                                44.Narrator: *She may have succeeded, but you... Your prince has been sacrificed, and you have been abandoned by your queen.*ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPathEND_General_State_CompKarlachIsMindFlayerLae'zel

                                Flags and Tags

                                checktype Global
                                • ORI_Laezel_State_IsOnOrpheusPath {97244ccc-a6b5-403e-9cfe-a3502eb4af56} = True

                                Metadata

                                Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 0f022184-15a2-cdac-f7de-ec15caa969c6Speaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: h27215531gc60eg4e61gaeb0gc6fe91647e48Text UUID: 595d899d-2624-468d-bca5-96d67ec0bacbTimeline Phase Index: 198Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 0f022184-15a2-cdac-f7de-ec15caa969c6Timeline Phase Duration: 8.84sTimeline Phase Shots: 2Timeline Location Changes: Landing - Player w Mindflayer Karlach (5528e48e-29e6-4a02-9ad9-8950f9ae3768) @ 1861.39s
                                • +
                                  724.Narrator: *The threat of the Grand Design is over, but your people will never accept you back into their embrace.*

                                  Metadata

                                  Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: ead2a1bd-994a-7b4a-b521-82c0d4d2bc31Speaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: h88b826b3g18a8g4e7cgb116gab8b53883061Text UUID: 298d6ef0-9148-4451-a093-53d124259657Timeline Phase Index: 95Timeline Dialog Node UUID: ead2a1bd-994a-7b4a-b521-82c0d4d2bc31Timeline Phase Duration: 7.24sTimeline Phase Shots: 2Timeline Location Changes: 342b9433-5258-4414-8f7f-6cdc312a52e8 (342b9433-5258-4414-8f7f-6cdc312a52e8) @ 870.34s
                                  • +
                                    253.Narrator: *You are a mind flayer, the very thing you were taught to hate. Whatever self you still possess is quickly ebbing away.*Githyanki!END_BrainBattle_Event_PlayerEmbracedBeingMindflayerFull-IllithidEND_BrainBattle_Event_PlayerEmbracedBeingMindflayer

                                    Flags and Tags

                                    ruletagtype rule
                                    • Githyanki {e49c027c-6ec6-4158-9afb-8b59236d10fd}
                                    checktype Global
                                    • END_BrainBattle_Event_PlayerEmbracedBeingMindflayer {dbfa834e-5913-0825-58b2-edf48b154a06} = False
                                    checktype Tag | speaker 0 Player
                                    • Full-Illithid {3797bfc4-8004-4a19-9578-61ce0714cc0b} = True
                                    settype Global
                                    • END_BrainBattle_Event_PlayerEmbracedBeingMindflayer {dbfa834e-5913-0825-58b2-edf48b154a06} = True

                                    Text

                                    Variant 1

                                    combine=0
                                    • Githyanki
                                    *You are a mind flayer, the very thing you were taught to hate. Whatever self you still possess is quickly ebbing away.*
                                    hfc8f7672gc224g417fga472gb04fa0105172 | cc955970-c452-486a-8b16-825afa820d06 | stub

                                    Variant 2

                                    *You are a mind flayer, the very thing you sought to eradicate. Whatever self you still possess is quickly ebbing away.*
                                    hf891d7bdg04d5g4369gb247g9ef1985b47ac | d96f0620-5921-4f45-a204-05e36efccf8e | stub

                                    Metadata

                                    Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 50d2ebb2-c14c-95e8-8c8b-0e7172175941Speaker: -666 (Narrator)Text 1 Handle: hfc8f7672gc224g417fga472gb04fa0105172Text 1 UUID: cc955970-c452-486a-8b16-825afa820d06Text 2 Handle: hf891d7bdg04d5g4369gb247g9ef1985b47acText 2 UUID: d96f0620-5921-4f45-a204-05e36efccf8eTimeline Phase Index: 303Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 50d2ebb2-c14c-95e8-8c8b-0e7172175941Timeline Phase Duration: 10.05sTimeline Phase Shots: 2
                                    • +
                                      554.Narrator: *Your friends and enemies alike are ripe for manipulation, and if not manipulation, then consumption.*

                                      Metadata

                                      Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: aeb168d5-9e55-bc82-12c3-d3708a4e84ddSpeaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: h5c118789g5099g4426ga5fdg7e1b1a5550b8Text UUID: 4dcba747-f80e-4696-a12a-b2c262ddc771Timeline Phase Index: 26Timeline Dialog Node UUID: aeb168d5-9e55-bc82-12c3-d3708a4e84ddTimeline Phase Duration: 9.48sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                                      • +
                                        629.Narrator: *Soon you will not be able to trust yourself at all. You will be a monstrosity beyond redemption.*

                                        Metadata

                                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: cbdda5cc-0b3a-0279-1f07-99a2d0049f6aSpeaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: had210d32g9268g41ccg9073g033c68168299Text UUID: 5e8a3e0c-2032-4952-ae6b-242fbe1f8c3cTimeline Phase Index: 153Timeline Dialog Node UUID: cbdda5cc-0b3a-0279-1f07-99a2d0049f6aTimeline Phase Duration: 8.31sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                                        • +
                                          591.Narrator: *Or not. Perhaps you are unique among illithid-kind. Perhaps you will retain enough of who you are to resist your nature.*

                                          Metadata

                                          Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: bc5e800e-9e81-33a9-6ac6-31904c07661aSpeaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: hf6679ab8g0f88g4475gaf1ag7cb363d456ebText UUID: 4ae7f194-b03f-4bd1-9ee9-643636ede7acTimeline Phase Index: 129Timeline Dialog Node UUID: bc5e800e-9e81-33a9-6ac6-31904c07661aTimeline Phase Duration: 9.47sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                                          • +
                                            368.Narrator: *A rogue mind flayer. Like the Emperor.*

                                            Metadata

                                            Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: 76d31e7e-83ab-6fa7-e6ba-5c1ba89355cdSpeaker: -666 (Narrator)Text Handle: h4547d5ffgd6d7g4185g9ce9g50b8d68f9277Text UUID: 4173e56c-37f1-4bcc-8a83-7d96dfb5258aTimeline Phase Index: 258Timeline Dialog Node UUID: 76d31e7e-83ab-6fa7-e6ba-5c1ba89355cdTimeline Phase Duration: 3.68sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                                            • +

                                              Metadata

                                              • +

                                          143.Origin_Intro_Laezel: Zhak vo'n'fynh duj - source of my joy, I wish...ORI_Laezel_State_CompletedRomanceFull-IllithidEND_GameFinale_State_LaezelInFaerun

                                          • +
                                            123.Origin_Intro_Laezel: No, I will not hesitate - this is my home. My destiny is for neither Vlaakith nor Orpheus to decree. It is mine.

                                            Metadata

                                          • +
                                            13.Origin_Intro_Laezel: But you are ghaik. As noble as you are, this is a threshold I cannot cross. This is where our fates diverge.

                                        97.Origin_Intro_Laezel: You helped our people end the Grand Design - for this, I will remember you. But I will not forget what you have cost us.Githyanki

                                        Flags and Tags

                                        ruletagtype rule

                                        • Githyanki

                                            720.Origin_Intro_Laezel: The world looms before me - never has it felt so vast, and so empty. But at least it will be a world in which there is no Grand Design. !END_CombatOver_State_LaezelInDialogEND_General_State_InclusionExcluded

                                                    229.Origin_Intro_Laezel: It is true you helped me end the Grand Design. But the path you chose has cost our people greatly. I will thank you, but I will not stay for you. Githyanki!END_CombatOver_State_LaezelInDialogEND_General_State_InclusionExcluded

                        413.Origin_Intro_Laezel: I have crossed blades with countless warriors among the Tears of Selûne. But none so resilient as you.END_RandomCompanion_PickedLae'zel

                        Flags and Tags

                        730.ShadowheartIllusion: This wouldn't have been possible without you. We'd all be the thralls of that thing, or worse. Instead, we're free.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedShadowheart

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 4 ShadowheartIllusion
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        162.Origin_Intro_Wyll: Before we met, some hailed me as a saviour, some as a legend. But now, I've seen the face of true heroism: yours.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedWyll

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 6 Origin_Intro_Wyll

                      • +
                        365.Gale: If it wasn't for you, we'd be thralls, dead, or who knows. This is your victory.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedGale

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 7 Gale
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        125.Halsin: This victory would not have been possible without you. I will never forget that.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedHalsin

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 9 Halsin

                      • +
                        302.Jaheira: They'll write stories of this, of course - each fable sprouting more falsehoods than the last. But for that one seed of truth: you. You did this.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedJaheira

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 10 Jaheira
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        • See Node 298 Jump to Node 413
                      • +
                        25.Speaker 11: I will be serious for a moment. Often Minsc speaks for his hamster, but now he speaks from his own heart, too - thank you. You saved us.END_RandomCompanion_PickedMinsc

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 11 Speaker 11
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True
                        checktype Tag | speaker 11 Speaker 11

                        122.Minthara: Without you, the brain would have enthralled me again. I would rather die... I owe you everything.END_RandomCompanion_PickedMinthara

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 12 Minthara
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        47.Astarion: Although credit where it's due - you were a worthy ally up there. If not for you, that brain may have bested us.ORI_Astarion_State_BecameVampireLordEND_RandomCompanion_PickedAstarion

                        680.Astarion: I'm glad you see it too. Baldur's Gate will never forget what you did - what we did.!ORI_Astarion_State_BecameVampireLordEND_RandomCompanion_PickedAstarion

                        Flags and Tags

                      • +
                        750.ShadowheartIllusion: Well, I'm certainly glad you feel that way - I doubt I could be so forward-thinking, in your place. Thank you.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedShadowheart

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 4 ShadowheartIllusion
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        • See Node 251 Jump to Node 95
                      • +
                        23.Origin_Intro_Wyll: You gave up so much so that others could walk free. I know sacrifice - and I must honour yours.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedWyll

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 6 Origin_Intro_Wyll
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        • See Node 251 Jump to Node 95
                      • +
                        698.Gale: You won't hear a syllable of disagreement from these lips. Thank you.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedGale

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 7 Gale
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        • See Node 251 Jump to Node 95
                      • +
                        148.Halsin: I believe so. Once the din of battle has faded, we will truly know what has been saved today.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedHalsin

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 9 Halsin
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True
                        checktype Tag | speaker 9 Halsin

                        • See Node 251 Jump to Node 95
                      • +
                        114.Jaheira: A simple creed to spout. A harder one to live by.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedJaheira

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 10 Jaheira
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        • See Node 251 Jump to Node 95
                      • +
                        116.Speaker 11: You wear the flayer's face, and yet your noble soul has not fled before it! I do not know if Boo or I would have your courage.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedMinsc

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 11 Speaker 11
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True
                        checktype Tag | speaker 11 Speaker 11
                        • Minsc {aeb694fc-83fb-452d-819a-b97ba442dc42} = True

                        Metadata

                        • See Node 251 Jump to Node 95
                      • +
                        711.Minthara: I doubt the world will appreciate your sacrifice. More likely, it will fear and shun you. But you have my thanks, for whatever they are worth.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedMinthara

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 12 Minthara
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        • See Node 251 Jump to Node 95

                        377.Origin_Intro_Laezel: I won't argue. I know - I can barely believe it myself. I will carve your name and deeds in tir'su, forever etched in slate. END_RandomCompanion_PickedLae'zel

                        Flags and Tags

                        312.ShadowheartIllusion: Everyone owes you, in truth. I'll see that all of Lady Shar's children know of their tentacled champion.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedChosen of SharShadowheart

                        END_RandomCompanion_Picked!Chosen of SharShadowheart

                        770.Astarion: For once I will happily admit it - I do. I owe you everything.!ORI_Astarion_State_BecameVampireLordEND_RandomCompanion_PickedAstarion

                        Flags and Tags

                        489.Origin_Intro_Wyll: No question. The people will sing psalms praising your name - and my voice will be the loudest among them.END_RandomCompanion_PickedWyll

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 6 Origin_Intro_Wyll

                        • See Node 635 Jump to Node 377
                      • +
                        651.Gale: Have a ponder on what your prize shall be then. I'm still a touch winded from it all...
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedGale

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 7 Gale

                      • +
                        411.Halsin: As does everyone. I have little doubt your efforts will be rewarded in whatever way you see fit.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedHalsin

                        Flags and Tags

                        • See Node 635 Jump to Node 377
                      • +
                        633.Jaheira: Myself and the Coast entire. A debt I'm unlikely to repay in the years left to me, but I suppose I can make the effort.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedJaheira

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 10 Jaheira

                        • See Node 635 Jump to Node 377
                      • +
                        713.Speaker 11: Minsc and Boo would recommend a statue on the Wide, but it is not always the honour it seems.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedMinsc

                        Flags and Tags

                        • See Node 635 Jump to Node 377
                      • +
                        295.Minthara: The whole world owes you, but expect their fear rather than their gratitude. You have my thanks though, for whatever they are worth.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedMinthara

                        Flags and Tags

                        396.Origin_Intro_Laezel: You faced impossible choices. You made the right one. Few would have the nerve.END_RandomCompanion_PickedLae'zel

                        Flags and Tags

                        150.ShadowheartIllusion: Not just that - you did what no one else was capable of. Thank you.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedShadowheart

                        Flags and Tags

                        739.Astarion: Indeed you did - I'll make sure the plebs of this city know who they should thank for their continued existence.ORI_Astarion_State_BecameVampireLordEND_RandomCompanion_PickedAstarion

                        364.Astarion: Baldur's Gate will never forget it. And neither will I.!ORI_Astarion_State_BecameVampireLordEND_RandomCompanion_PickedAstarion

                        Flags and Tags

                      • +
                        528.Origin_Intro_Wyll: You gave up so much so that others could walk free. I know sacrifice - and I must honour yours.END_RandomCompanion_PickedWyll

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 6 Origin_Intro_Wyll
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        • See Node 397 Jump to Node 396
                      • +
                        166.Gale: Oh, spare me the modesty. They should erect statues to you... though they may frighten children.END_RandomCompanion_PickedGale

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 7 Gale
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        • See Node 397 Jump to Node 396
                      • +
                        73.Halsin: So modest of you. Indeed, the mighty oak need not boast of its branches.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedHalsin

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 9 Halsin

                        • See Node 397 Jump to Node 396
                      • +
                        578.Jaheira: A simple creed to spout. A harder one to live by.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedJaheira

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 10 Jaheira
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        • See Node 397 Jump to Node 396
                      • +
                        553.Speaker 11: You wear the flayer's face, and yet your noble soul has not fled before it! I do not know if Boo or I would have your courage.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedMinsc

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 11 Speaker 11
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True
                        checktype Tag | speaker 11 Speaker 11
                        • Minsc {aeb694fc-83fb-452d-819a-b97ba442dc42} = True

                        Metadata

                        Node Type: TagAnswerUUID: ae4c366b-dbd3-3464-c2dc-19a3a36bc46dSpeaker: 11 (Speaker 11)Text Handle: h39437dd2g9425g4504g8a1fg5b23a9928fcdText UUID: 98e36bc3-0f6b-499f-a8ee-e7aaa3ae71bcTimeline Phase Index: 27Timeline Dialog Node UUID: ae4c366b-dbd3-3464-c2dc-19a3a36bc46dTimeline Phase Duration: 9.45sTimeline Phase Shots: 1
                        • See Node 397 Jump to Node 396
                      • +
                        49.Minthara: And yet it was you who did it. The world will not thank you - it will fear you - but you have my gratitude, for whatever its worth to you.
                        END_RandomCompanion_PickedMinthara

                        Flags and Tags

                        checktype Object | speaker 12 Minthara
                        • END_RandomCompanion_Picked {d39d2a4e-2efb-c14c-ba23-f5c03fac5038} = True

                        • See Node 397 Jump to Node 396
                  • +
                    700.Player: It's all right. I like what I've become.Full-Illithid

                      485.Origin_Intro_Laezel: You are ghaik. By all accounts, I should hate the very sight of you. But I know the heart that beats within. It belongs to a warrior.END_RandomCompanion_PickedLae'zel
